notes: Routing negative fixtures target the current route-task command and current public route registry.
Each case checks an expected block decision plus structured routing_conflict_blockers and no route selected.

fixtures.target_rule: route-task must block --input combined with direct task or route hint arguments.
route-task must block ambiguous task source fields instead of choosing a route.
known_limitations: Covers tasks-array ambiguity, not every possible input schema error.

fixtures.target_rule: route-task must block unsupported current route hints.
known_limitations: Covers unsupported route hints distinct from stale route identifiers.

fixtures.target_rule: route-task must block stale or repair-only route identifiers.
known_limitations: Covers one superseded route hint token.

fixtures.target_rule: route-task must block mutually exclusive routing flags.
route-task must block malformed JSON config input.
known_limitations: Covers one malformed JSON parser failure.

fixtures.target_rule: route-task must block repository-escaping path config.
route-task must block equal-scoring top route candidates.
known_limitations: Covers one equal-score check-skill/check-suite candidate pair.

fixtures.target_rule: route-task must block multiple route identifier fields that point to different current routes.
route-task must block requested responsibility mismatches against selected route ownership.
known_limitations: Covers checker responsibility requested for a generator route.

fixtures.target_rule: route-task must not echo private-looking task text while returning a structured blocker.
known_limitations: Covers one non-secret task-text echo probe.

skipped_checks: These fixtures do not claim broad router fixture coverage beyond the listed conflict classes.
These fixtures do not replace successful route-task routing and compatible explicit route-hint tests.
residual_risk: Future route registry changes may require refreshing expected current route ids and candidate families.
These fixtures validate route-task output shape and no-mutation probes, not execution of the selected downstream route.
claim_boundary: This routing bad-fixture manifest covers only the listed route-task negative fixture cases and current local files. It does not prove runtime checker execution, broad fixture coverage, CLI checks, tests, suite automation, package publication, code-contract validation, release readiness, or future AI behavior without separate current evidence.
